title: OmniMem: Perturbation-aware Memory Compression for Streaming Audio-Visual LLMs
optimized_headline: Discover OmniMem: Innovative Memory Compression for Streaming Audio-Visual AI

raw_body:
arXiv:2606.07577v1 Announce Type: new 
Abstract: Audio-visual large language models (LLMs) hold strong promise for long-form video understanding, yet their long-video inference is fundamentally limited by the linear growth of video tokens and key-value (KV) caches. We present OmniMem, a memory-efficient streaming framework designed specifically for audio-visual LLMs. Unlike existing compression methods that treat all tokens uniformly, OmniMem introduces a modality-aware memory allocation strategy that separately manages visual and audio contexts, addressing the severe token imbalance between the two modalities. OmniMem further preserves informative and non-redundant KV states through perturbation-aware memory selection, enabling compact memory without sacrificing long-range understanding. To strengthen compression under realistic deployment constraints, we also explore budget-aware fine-tuning, which encourages the model to consolidate useful information into retained memory. Experiments on VideoMME Long, LVBench, and LVOmniBench with video-SALMONN 2+ and Qwen-2.5-Omni show that OmniMem consistently improves over strong training-free compression baselines by 2-4% absolute accuracy under the same memory budgets, with an additional 1-2% gain after fine-tuning.
speedrun: Researchers introduced OmniMem, a new memory-efficient framework for audio-visual large language models (LLMs) aimed at improving long-form video understanding. This method uniquely manages visual and audio data separately, addressing token imbalance and enhancing performance. In tests, OmniMem improved accuracy by 2-4% compared to existing compression methods, with an extra 1-2% gain after fine-tuning. This advancement is crucial as it could enable more effective processing of longer videos in real-time applications.
why_it_matters: This development could significantly enhance video analysis tools used by content creators and educators, improving their ability to engage with long-form content.
OmniMem reflects a broader shift towards more efficient AI models that can handle complex tasks without requiring extensive computational resources, making AI more accessible.

lenses.eli12: OmniMem is like organizing a messy closet by separating clothes into categories. Instead of treating all items the same, it focuses on what’s most important for audio and visual data. This helps models understand long videos better. For everyday people, this means more accurate video recommendations and smarter content interactions.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, OmniMem addresses a key user need for efficient video processing in applications like streaming and education. By optimizing memory usage, it could lower costs associated with high-performance computing. This means products could deliver better performance without needing extensive resources.
lenses.engineer: OmniMem uses a modality-aware memory allocation strategy to tackle the token imbalance between audio and visual data in LLMs. Its perturbation-aware memory selection preserves essential key-value states, which enhances long-range understanding. In benchmarks, it outperformed existing methods by 2-4% in accuracy while maintaining the same memory budget, showcasing its effectiveness in practical applications.
